title: genista boissieri subsp. webbii
intro: Genista boissieri subsp. webbii, also known as Webb's broom, is a subspecies of shrub belonging to the legume family. It is a small, deciduous shrub native to the Mediterranean region, known for its bright yellow flowers and medicinal properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers are used in herbal medicine, attributed with antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate respiratory issues. Genista boissieri subsp. webbii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive problems and skin irritations.
